3.blurb: Formal computation, data structures, and the first vocabulary of machine learning.
skills: Reason formally about computation using automata, grammars and Turing machines
Implement and analyze core data structures — trees, graphs, hash tables — for time/space efficiency
Design and simulate digital logic circuits at the gate and register-transfer level
Apply probability, random variables and stochastic processes to engineering problems
Describe the supervised/unsupervised learning paradigms that later ML courses build on
Frame technical decisions with an awareness of ethics, sustainability and economics

4.blurb: Classical AI, operating-system internals, and databases, with the first taste of specialization.
skills: Implement classical AI search, knowledge representation and reasoning techniques
Explain and modify OS internals — process scheduling, memory management, concurrency
Design relational and NoSQL schemas and query them effectively
Apply graph theory to model real-world systems
Go one layer deeper in a chosen elective track — software engineering, pattern recognition, computer architecture, or similar
Build, debug and document multi-component lab projects (OS + AI labs)

5.blurb: Neural networks, computer vision, and networking, alongside a widening elective basket.
skills: Train and evaluate deep neural networks from first principles
Build basic computer-vision pipelines: image processing, features, recognition
Explain the TCP/IP protocol stack and analyze network performance
Design algorithms with formal complexity/correctness analysis (DAA)
Specialize further via electives — compilers, fuzzy/expert systems, signal processing, or data mining
Plan and manage a software project's scope, schedule and risk

6.blurb: Language models, generative AI, and data analytics — the first semester with Open Electives.
skills: Build NLP pipelines: tokenization, embeddings, sequence models, and transformer-based methods
Work hands-on with generative AI architectures covered in the Generative AI course
Run an end-to-end data analytics workflow from raw data to insight
Choose across a cross-department Open Elective basket (data structures, crypto, ML for engineers, AI, networking) in addition to a Program Elective
Evaluate an emerging computing paradigm in depth — cloud, quantum, or cryptographic systems — depending on the elective picked

7.blurb: An almost entirely elective semester — pick a lane and go deep.
skills: Apply deep, self-chosen specialization knowledge from a 19-course Program Elective basket spanning security, theory, systems, AI ethics and NLP
Translate research-level topics — formal methods, approximation algorithms, parallel computing — into working solutions
Practice responsible/ethical AI design and evaluate systems against fairness and safety criteria, if that elective is chosen
Build systems-level depth in embedded, real-time, or high-performance computing tracks
Pick a second, cross-department Open Elective to broaden outside the core AI/ML track

8.blurb: Final electives in LLMs, security and HCI — paired, in the official scheme, with a major project.
skills: Synthesize coursework into a capstone-level individual specialization — LLMs, security, HCI, storage systems, or similar
Apply prompt-engineering and LLM techniques to real generative-AI products
Reason about complex, large-scale software/AI systems end-to-end
Round out the degree with a final Open Elective outside the core track
Demonstrate readiness for industry or research — typically alongside a Major Project credited outside this syllabus document
